question.label: Escalation judgment
question.name: What kind of call did you hold too long before escalating this quarter?
question.description: Probes decision-making under uncertainty and calibration of when to pull in help.

answer: Specific feedback checkpoints during projects would help me level up faster.

assessment.note: Names missing feedback checkpoints as the cause of slower leveling, which is a strong clarity deficit and mild growth gap.

new_queue.label: Next-quarter focus
new_queue.name: Given what we've covered, what do you want to work on first over the next quarter?

issues: coverage: injected wellbeing into next question (0 touches after turn 8)

history.answer_excerpt: I think my technical output is solid, but my influence is uneven.
I need a tighter narrative on tradeoffs, not just stronger models.
I think my technical output is solid, but my influence is uneven.
I get work done, yet I am not consistently shaping decisions early.
Some feedback has been about being clearer with non-technical partners.
I tend to wait until analysis is complete before socializing direction.
That slows trust because stakeholders feel surprised late.
I need a tighter narrative on tradeoffs, not just stronger models.
I want to lead one cross-team decision this cycle, end to end.
Specific feedback checkpoints during projects would help me level up faster.
